title: Relations, Rows, Domains, Keys, and Missing Information · Result-Grain Case: Foreign Referential Keys
summary: Support tickets, teams, members, and events must become a precise relational model without duplicate identity or invented missing values. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql foreign referential keys increment in a key-and-null-aware relational model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model optional and required references with foreign keys and predict update or delete consequences.
Distinguish superkeys, candidate keys, primary keys, alternate keys, and stable surrogate identifiers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Model teams, members, tickets, and events where members may be unassigned, ticket titles repeat, and one ticket has 4 events. Declare row grain, candidate keys, cardinalities, NULL meaning, dependencies, and lossless relationships. A defensible solution applies Foreign Referential Keys, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a key-and-null-aware relational model.

content.code:
SELECT t.ticket_id, t.owner_id, m.name AS owner_name
FROM tickets AS t
LEFT JOIN members AS m ON m.member_id = t.owner_id
WHERE t.owner_id IS NULL OR m.active = 1
ORDER BY t.ticket_id;
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

GROUP BY team_id
ORDER BY team_id;
